title: Network Egress Minimization
description: Reduce cloud egress cost by co-locating compute and data, using CDNs, and avoiding cross-region data flows.

cost.costNotes: Estimates are intentionally conservative; verify against current pricing before committing.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Network Egress Minimization is an optimization pattern that reduces costs rather than incurring them. It involves architectural decisions (data locality, CDN usage, avoiding cross-region transfers) that decrease egress charges. The pattern itself has no direct infrastructure cost - it's applied to existing services. Actual savings depend on current egress volume: organizations can save $100-$10,000+/month depending on data transfer patterns. CDN costs (if newly adopted) would be $20-$500/month but typically offset by egress savings. Setting to $0 as this is a cost-reduction pattern, not a cost-incurring service.
provenance.adoptionCost: Medium-high complexity implementation. Requires: (1) Architecture review to identify egress patterns and hotspots (8-16 hours, $800-$1,600), (2) Redesigning data flows and API patterns to enforce locality (16-32 hours, $1,200-$2,400), (3) CDN configuration and testing if not already in use (8-16 hours, $400-$800), (4) Monitoring setup for egress metrics (4-8 hours, $200-$400), (5) Documentation and team training on egress-aware design principles (4-8 hours, $200-$400). Total: 40-80 hours at $50-$100/hour = $2,000-$8,000. Mid-range estimate of $2,500 reflects that some organizations may already have partial CDN coverage or monitoring, reducing implementation time. Higher end applies when significant architectural refactoring is needed across multiple services.
provenance.licenseCost: No licensing costs required. This pattern uses native cloud provider features (regional resource placement, VPC peering, CDN services) that are usage-based, not license-based. Open-source monitoring tools (Prometheus, Grafana, CloudWatch) can track egress metrics. Commercial network monitoring tools exist ($5,000-$50,000/year) but are optional - native cloud dashboards provide sufficient visibility for most implementations. CDNs like CloudFront, Azure CDN, or Cloudflare operate on pay-per-use models without upfront licensing fees.

provides.reasoning: This pattern directly reduces network egress costs through strategic architectural decisions like co-locating compute with data, using CDNs, and avoiding cross-region transfers. The core purpose of this pattern is to decrease billable egress traffic through architectural optimization.
Co-locating compute and data resources is a core technique of this pattern. This capability enables reduced latency and lower egress costs by keeping data processing close to data storage.
CDN usage is explicitly mentioned as a key technique in this pattern for reducing egress costs by caching content closer to end users and minimizing origin server traffic.

requires.reasoning: To implement this pattern effectively, you need the ability to control and configure network topology, including region placement, VPC configuration, and routing decisions to avoid cross-region data flows.
This pattern requires visibility into egress costs to identify optimization opportunities and measure the effectiveness of implemented strategies.

Identifying high-egress data flows is essential for knowing which systems and data paths need optimization. Traffic analysis enables data-driven decisions about where to apply co-location and CDN strategies.

Managing compute and data placement strategies across geographic regions is fundamental to implementing this pattern. You need control over where resources are deployed to achieve co-location and minimize cross-region transfers.

supports_nfr.reason: Egress minimization through regional co-location and CDN integration adds architectural complexity; systems with low availability targets may not justify this overhead.
At very low request volumes, network egress costs are minimal and the complexity of egress optimization (CDN setup, regional co-location) may not be cost-justified.
Egress cost reduction through CDN and regional co-location is compatible with GDPR compliance requirements.
Cost optimization strategies through data locality work with or without CCPA compliance requirements.
Egress minimization patterns are neutral to SOX compliance requirements for financial data.

supports_constraints.reason: Network egress costs and optimization strategies are specific to major cloud providers with metered cross-region bandwidth.
Egress minimization applies to any platform that serves data to external clients or transfers data across network boundaries.
Network egress optimization is an infrastructure and topology pattern that operates independently of application language.
Egress reduction benefits from caching but does not require it as a prerequisite.
Cost optimization through data locality applies to both synchronous and asynchronous communication patterns.
Egress minimization through regional co-location works for both single-tenant and multi-tenant architectures.
Data locality strategies for cost reduction apply to batch processing and real-time workloads.
Network topology optimization for egress cost is independent of distributed transaction requirements.
Egress cost reduction benefits streaming workloads through co-location but applies to non-streaming workloads as well.
Infrastructure-level egress optimization is neutral to the presence of vector search capabilities.
Network cost optimization strategies apply regardless of document store usage.
Egress minimization through data locality works with any storage backend including key-value stores.
Cost reduction through network topology optimization is independent of graph database usage.
Egress cost optimization applies to time-series and non-time-series workloads equally.
Network egress minimization benefits OLTP workloads but is not limited to transactional systems.
Data locality and CDN strategies for cost reduction apply to analytical and transactional workloads.

cdn_strategy.description: CDN usage level. 'full' caches both static and dynamic content at edge (lowest egress, highest complexity), 'static-only' caches assets only (balanced), 'none' for internal-only apps (no CDN cost but higher egress)

data_locality.description: Data placement strategy. 'global' replicates worldwide (lowest latency, highest cost), 'regional' keeps data in user regions (balanced), 'single-zone' centralizes data (lowest cost, higher latency for distant users)

cross_region_replication.description: Cross-region data sync level. 'none' avoids all cross-region traffic (lowest egress cost), 'minimal' syncs only critical data (balanced), 'full' maintains complete replicas (highest availability, highest cost)

compression_enabled.description: Enable compression for data transfers. Reduces egress volume by 60-80% but adds CPU overhead. Recommended for most workloads.

static_asset_strategy.description: Static asset delivery approach. 'cdn_edge' serves from CDN edge locations (lowest egress from origin), 'regional_storage' uses regional buckets (moderate cost), 'origin_only' serves directly (highest egress cost, simplest setup)
